definition: R-star uncertainty of ±100-150bp propagates into ±15-20% equity fair value uncertainty via Gordon Growth Model sensitivity (~3-4 P/E turns per 100bp). The inconsistency between 5y5y forwards (pricing r-star 1.5-2.0%) and equity multiples (pricing r-star 0.5-1.0%) makes the valuation framework fundamentally underdetermined.
evidence: Gordon Growth Model sensitivity is mechanical arithmetic
5y5y forwards vs equity multiples inconsistency is directly observable
Pair_1 debate identified this as the most important single insight
If you don't know whether equities are fairly valued or 15-20% overvalued, most other risk factors are second-order
open_questions: Which r-star estimate should the framework anchor to?
Does the 5y5y vs equity multiple divergence resolve toward bonds or equities?
How should other risk factors be weighted given this dominant source of uncertainty?
